Scenario.title: APT41 DUST Attack Scenario
Scenario.overview: Energy Sector Infrastructure Simple This scenario depicts a targeted attack against energy sector organizations, leveraging phishing for initial compromise followed by command execution and data extraction.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Email Systems, Web Servers, User Workstations
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Phishing, Social Engineering, Malicious Link Clicking

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: An adversary initiates contact through deceptive emails designed to trick recipients into revealing credentials or clicking malicious links. These emails may appear legitimate, exploiting trust relationships within the energy sector.

stage.name: Execution
stage.capec: CAPEC-9: Buffer Overflow in Local Command-Line Utilities
stage.description: Upon successful phishing, the attacker executes malicious commands on the compromised endpoint, potentially leveraging vulnerabilities in command-line tools to run unauthorized scripts or binaries, escalating privileges or accessing restricted areas.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.description: Infered from typical APT behaviors, the attacker establishes persistence by creating a scheduled task linked to their command-and-control server, ensuring ongoing access to the energy network endpoints.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: Exploiting weaknesses in authentication protocols, the attacker elevates privileges to gain domain admin rights, facilitating broader control over the energy sector's IT and OT environments.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.description: To evade detection, the attacker uses process impersonation to mask malicious activities within legitimate processes, blending with normal traffic on energy sector networks.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The final stage involves gathering and preparing sensitive data for exfiltration, potentially causing operational disruption by overwhelming systems with large data transfers aimed at stealing intellectual property or grid control information.

Scenario.summary: A straightforward attack chain beginning with phishing to breach defenses, progressing through command execution and evasion tactics, culminating in data theft impacting energy sector operations.
